'''''Mal'ary'ush''''' was [[Honoghran]] for ''daughter of the savior.'' The ''savior'' in question was [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], whom the [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] people revered. [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] earlier, a [[Gahenna|starship]] carrying noxious chemicals [[Battle of Honoghr/Legends|crashed]] on [[Honoghr/Legends|Honoghr]] and threatened to destroy the entire [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. They believed [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] sent Vader to the planet to help them, but in reality Palpatine was responsible for the devastation of Honoghr, as a way to bend their allegiance to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

The ''daughter'' was [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], who years later would find herself at the mercy of the Noghri people in the Republic's [[Thrawn campaign|battle]] against their new master, [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]]. However, one Noghri, [[Khabarakh]], through the use of the sense of smell inherent to the Noghri—acute enough to smell even one's bloodline—discovered she was the daughter of Vader and pledged to help her escape Imperial custody. Later, Leia would [[Mission to Honoghr|reveal]] the treachery of Palpatine to the Noghri, which emboldened them to finally [[Noghri revolution|break]] their allegiance to the Galactic Empire.
